topic.title: Model Badging: SRM Platform Identity from an IIC Discriminator Device

header.para: Model Badging: SRM Platform Identity from an IIC Discriminator Device

body.para: The SRM firmware brands each machine with a platform name in its power-up banner ("AlphaServer DS20", "AlphaPC 264DP", and so on). That name is not configured anywhere in EmulatR -- the firmware derives it at boot from the member id in the HWRPB SYSVAR word, and it computes that member by probing the IIC bus for a model-specific discriminator device. For EmulatR to badge a model correctly, its platform manifest must populate the exact IIC device that model's firmware probes. This topic states the general pattern, the DS20 case (root-caused 2026-06-30), and how to diagnose badging for any model.

para.text: This topic supersedes "DS20 Badge: Polled IIC Completion". That earlier thesis -- a missing polled IIC completion times out the node-0x40 verify -- was disproven: the completion is delivered correctly, node 0x40 reads cleanly, and the badge never depended on node 0x40 at all.

body.para: Symptom

The banner shows the base / wrong model. The HWRPB scan reports SYSTYPE=0x22 (DEC_TSUNAMI) and SYSVAR=0x405. Member = (SYSVAR >> 10) & 0x3F = 1, which the SRM banners as "AlphaPC 264DP". The correct DS20 is member 6 (SYSVAR=0x1805), bannered "AlphaServer DS20".
How the SRM computes the badge

build_hwrpb calls get_sysvar() (pc264.c:636). get_sysvar starts with the base sysvar = HWRPB$_SYSVAR = 0x5, then attempts fopen() on a model-specific IIC device; on success it ORs member << 10 into SYSVAR<15:10>, else it takes the member-1 default. The value is stored to the HWRPB SYSVAR word at physical address 0x2058.

Retire-trace of the running V7.3-2 binary confirms the exact chain: build_hwrpb stores base 0x5 to PA 0x2058 (pc 0x5c3e0), calls get_sysvar (pc 0x7f5c0), which byte-walks the discriminator device name and on failure branches at BEQ pc=0x7f608 (R16 == 0) to load the member-1 default 0x405 as a literal (pc 0x7f660); build_hwrpb then stores 0x405 to PA 0x2058 (pc 0x5c3f8).
Member map (SYSTYPE 0x22, DEC_TSUNAMI / PC264)

table.style: border-width:0px; border-spacing:0px; border-collapse:collapse; cell-border-width:1px; border-color:#000000; border-style:solid; head-row-background-color:#00ffff; alt-row-background-color:none;

td.para: none -- reached when the others fail to open

body.para: Root cause (DS20)

The DS20 discriminator is iic_rcm_temp -- the temperature sensor on the KCRCM server-management card, which per the get_sysvar source comment is "only present on the AlphaServer DS20". It is a static node-table entry at IIC node 0x9e (iic_driver.c:264, IIC_LED_TYPE). EmulatR's IicPcf8584 populates its bus from the platform manifest; any node absent from the manifest NAKs. The DS20 manifest did not list node 0x9e, so the bus scan NAK'd it (IIC-TXN addr=0x9e dir=R -> NAK), iic_init never registered iic_rcm_temp, fopen("iic_rcm_temp") returned NULL, and get_sysvar fell to the member-1 default.
Why node 0x40 (iic_ocp0) was a red herring

An earlier analysis, and an earlier manifest fix, targeted iic_ocp0 @ 0x40, believing it the discriminator. It is not. iic_ocp0 is the OCP LED panel, present on both AlphaPC 264DP and AlphaServer DS20, so it cannot distinguish them. The reference apisrm get_sysvar code reads fopen("iic_ocp0"), but its own comment and the shipped V7.3-2 binary use the temperature sensor. The retire-trace of the running binary is authoritative: in the entire get_sysvar window it byte-walks iic_rcm_temp and never references iic_ocp0. Decisive proof: node 0x40 was already populated and read cleanly in every trace -- had it been the gate, the badge would already have been member 6.
Resolution
Populate the model's discriminator device in its platform manifest. For DS20, add one entry to ds20_v7_3_platform.json under iic_devices:
{ "name": "iic_rcm_temp", "address": "0x9e", "class": "status", "byte": "0x19" }

With node 0x9e ACKing, iic_init's verify read succeeds, iic_rcm_temp registers, fopen succeeds, and get_sysvar returns member 6 -> SYSVAR=0x1805 -> "AlphaServer DS20". Registration gates on the read succeeding (rec_count), not on the byte value, so byte 0x19 (~25 C) is cosmetic. The platform manifest is loaded at runtime -- no rebuild; edit the source manifest and sync the run-dir copy. Do not add iic_8574_ocp (0x4E) -- that yields member 8 = DS20E / Goldrack.
How to diagnose model badging
Arm the retire window on the base-SYSVAR store so the get_sysvar path is captured, and watch the SYSVAR word directly:
unset EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_ON_IIC # do not consume the window at the ~185M IIC read
export EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_PA=0x2058 # arm on the HWRPB SYSVAR store...
export EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_VAL=0x5 # ...value 0x5 (base), just before get_sysvar
export EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_INSTRS=2000000 # window over get_sysvar
export EMULATR_GMEM_WATCH=0x2058 # log every store to the SYSVAR word
export EMULATR_IIC_TRACE=1 # log IIC START / device reads + NAKs

Read it as follows. The GMEM-WATCH(0x2058) final store value encodes the member (0x405=1, 0x1805=6, 0x2005=8). In the retire .trc between the base (0x5) and final store, read which device name get_sysvar byte-walks (the strlen loop) -- that is the discriminator the running firmware actually probes. Cross-check its IIC node against the bus scan: an IIC-TXN ... -> NAK at that node means EmulatR does not populate the device.
General rule

Every model's SRM brands itself by probing for a signature IIC device that is unique to that model. To badge a new model correctly: trace the running binary's get_sysvar (the trace, not just the reference source, which can disagree with the shipped image), identify the device name and its IIC node, and populate a matching device at that node in the model's platform manifest. If the banner shows the base / wrong model, the discriminator node is NAKing -- add it.
See Also. DS20 Badge: Polled IIC Completion [SUPERSEDED]; Diagnostics: GuestMemory Store-Watch and Retire-Trace Arm; HWRPB - DS20; Virtualization Methods.
